reviews.reason: The theatre timeline accurately encodes early animal-cell differentiation and lifelong potential in many plant cells as a distinct visual contrast.
The job-toolkit image accurately links acquired sub-cellular structures to specialised function and provides a useful visual retrieval route.
The labelled-safe image accurately encodes nuclear enclosure and contrasts it with bacterial genetic material without a nucleus.
The two R-labelled drawers provide a grounded sound-and-space cue for the two main roles of mature-animal cell division.
The vacuum-flask association and contents label form a concrete mnemonic for cell sap filling the permanent plant-cell vacuole.
The target and central bullseye accurately encode the scattering conclusion that atomic mass is concentrated in the nucleus.
The zoom-and-stop image gives a concrete endpoint cue for the supported definition of an atom as the smallest existing part of an element.
The dartboard rings accurately encode Bohr's model of electrons orbiting the nucleus at specific distances.
The padlocked-recipe image accurately combines the two supported features: chemical combination and fixed proportions.
The guest-list image clearly encodes inclusion of protons and neutrons and exclusion of electrons from mass number.
The shared-room, marriage and badge imagery accurately encodes both defining mixture features from the cited evidence.
The ledger supplies a concrete balance image for equal electron and proton numbers producing no overall charge.
The pudding-and-fruit image accurately preserves the model's positive ball and embedded negative electrons as a useful spatial encoding.
The red gate clearly encodes the supported boundary condition that the proportionality limit must not have been exceeded.
